title: Stereotaxic virus injection in the SNpc of rats
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9qqgt","text":"Protocol for stereotaxic virus injection in the substantia nigra pars compacta of rats","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"duqvt","text":"This procedure was carried out following the guidelines on the ethical use of animals from the Council Directive of the European Communities (2010/63/EU) and were approved by the Italian Ministry of Health.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Anesthetize the rat with xylazine (Rompun, 20 mg/ml; 0.5 ml/kg; Bayer) and tiletamine/zolazepam (Zoletil, 100 mg/ml; 0.5 ml/kg; Virbac)
Mount the rat in a stereotaxic frame equipped with a rat adapter and stereotaxically inject into the SNpc (AP = -4.9 mm, ML=1.8 ± mm, DV = -7 mm) with the viral vectors (2 μl for side, 0.4 μL/min)
Left the needle in place for an additional 5 min to prevent backflow
creator: Ada Ledonne
